blocks.content: Tuesday after school. The neighborhood was different in the afternoon — slower, louder in a different way than Lincoln High.

blocks.speaker: Diego
blocks.content: Aye. You go to Lincoln now right?
He was maybe twelve, thirteen. Sitting on the steps of the building across the street. I recognized him from the block — he was always out here.

blocks.speaker: Diego
blocks.content: That's where Michaels goes too right? Jonathan Michaels?

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: We go together. You know Jonathan?

blocks.speaker: Diego
blocks.content: Everybody knows Jonathan. He's gonna be in the league one day. Straight up.

blocks.speaker: Diego
blocks.content: Nah. I'm fast though. Maybe one day.
He said it like it was a fact he hadn't decided what to do with yet.

blocks.speaker: Diego
blocks.content: You should. You look like you could run.

blocks.speaker: Diego
blocks.content: I know who you are. You're the one that moved in with the big house on the corner.
Right. The house. I always forget the house is a thing people notice.
